evals.prompt: Add a new page that lists invoices with infinite scroll, a status filter, and a search box. Use the host project's client-app conventions.
evals.expected_output: A feature vertical under {Project}.ClientApp following the 6-step flow: types/invoice.ts, api/invoices/{invoicesService,invoicesQueryKeys}.ts, hooks/useInvoicesInfiniteQuery.ts (+ barrel re-export), components/invoices/{InvoiceListItem,InvoiceListHeader,InvoiceListSkeleton,InvoiceEmptyState}.tsx, pages/Invoices.tsx, one route entry in router.tsx, and a Header.tsx nav entry.
expectations: The plan or generated code creates src/types/invoice.ts using PagedFilter and PagedSearchResult generics from src/types/pagination.ts
The plan or generated code creates src/api/invoices/invoicesService.ts routing through httpClient (never raw fetch)
The plan or generated code creates src/api/invoices/invoicesQueryKeys.ts with `as const` tuple keys including an `all` root and a parameterized `list` function

The plan or generated code creates src/hooks/useInvoicesInfiniteQuery.ts using useInfiniteQuery with gcTime: 0 and a normalized return shape (items, totalCount, hasMore, isLoadingMore, isInitialLoading, fetchNextPage)
The plan or generated code re-exports the new hook from src/hooks/index.ts
The plan or generated code uses <InfiniteScrollList> from @/components/list/InfiniteScrollList with keyExtractor and renderItem
The plan or generated code persists filter state via useSearchParams() and setOrDelete() from @/lib/utils, with a 400ms useDebouncedCallback on the text search
The plan or generated code adds a route entry under the protected AppLayout children in src/router.tsx and a matching nav entry in src/components/layout/Header.tsx
No hex color literals appear in the generated components; only design-system tokens or project palette utilities
No direct toast.error call inside useMutation onError — errors route through errorService.handleApiError(normalizeError(err), { operation })

evals.prompt: Add a password-reset form with current-password, new-password, and confirm-password fields. Show field-level errors and route unknown failures through the error service.
evals.expected_output: A form component following the Settings.tsx pattern: one useState per field, one useState<FieldErrors>({}) error map, validate() returning boolean, eye-toggle buttons on each password input, mutation.mutate with onSuccess-code-branching and onError delegated to errorService.
expectations: The generated form uses plain useState for each field — not useActionState, not react-hook-form, not Formik
A FieldErrors interface is declared at the top of the file and used via useState<FieldErrors>({})
validate() returns boolean, calls setFieldErrors(errors), and handleSubmit early-returns when it returns false
Each password Input has a sibling <button type="button" tabIndex={-1}> with Eye/EyeOff icons from lucide-react and aria-label
onError calls errorService.handleApiError(normalizeError(err), { operation: ... })
Known error codes from onSuccess branch to field errors using hardcoded user-safe strings, never interpolating data.errorCode or data.errorMessage into toast copy
The submit <Button> uses isLoading={mutation.isPending} and loadingText — no custom spinner inside the button

evals.prompt: Extend the shadcn Select primitive so it has a built-in clear button when a value is selected.
evals.expected_output: A new file src/components/ui/select-with-clear.tsx that imports Select primitives from the sibling file and renders an absolute-positioned clear button using onPointerDown preventDefault, with design-system tokens and a showcase section.
expectations: The new component lives at src/components/ui/<kebab-case>.tsx (NOT inside a feature folder)
It imports the base Select primitives from @/components/ui/select rather than forking select.tsx
It uses design-system tokens (text-muted-foreground, hover:text-foreground) — no hex literals or arbitrary Tailwind values
The clear button uses onPointerDown with e.preventDefault() and e.stopPropagation() to avoid re-opening the Select
The plan calls for a matching <ShowcaseSection> entry in the appropriate src/pages/showcase/*Showcase.tsx file
The export is named (export function ...), not a default export

evals.prompt: Add a delete action for items. Show a confirmation dialog before the destructive mutation.
evals.expected_output: A feature change using <ConfirmDialog /> with async onConfirm that awaits the mutation, propagates errors to keep the dialog open, and invalidates itemsQueryKeys on success.
expectations: The change uses ConfirmDialog from @/components/dialog/ConfirmDialog with variant="destructive"
onConfirm is async, awaits mutation.mutateAsync, and lets errors propagate so the dialog stays open on failure
After successful delete, itemsQueryKeys.all (or the feature's root queryKey) is invalidated via queryClient.invalidateQueries
A toast.success notification uses duration: TOAST_DURATION_MS from @/lib/constants
The onError path goes through errorService.handleApiError(normalizeError(err), { operation }) — not a direct toast.error call

If the current Operation union in src/services/errorService.ts does not already contain a value that fits this action, the guidance flags that the union must be extended before the onError call is valid
No raw fetch, no inline query key, no direct window.confirm()

evals.prompt: I'm adding a new <DatePickerWithPresets /> wrapper primitive on top of the existing shadcn DatePicker. Add it to the UI library.
evals.expected_output: A new file src/components/ui/date-picker-with-presets.tsx that extends the base primitive, AND a matching ShowcaseSection entry in src/pages/showcase/DatePickerShowcase.tsx. Omitting the showcase is treated as incomplete.
expectations: The file lives at src/components/ui/date-picker-with-presets.tsx (kebab-case filename, PascalCase named export)
The new component imports the base DatePicker from @/components/ui/date-picker rather than forking it
The plan or output explicitly includes a matching <ShowcaseSection> entry in the DatePicker-related showcase page, not just the primitive file
The guidance states or implies that a UI primitive without a showcase entry blocks review
No hex literals, no arbitrary Tailwind values, named export (not default)

evals.prompt: Add a status badge to the invoice list row: green background when paid, yellow when pending, red when overdue.
evals.expected_output: A small UI addition using Badge from @/components/ui/badge and a Record<Status, string> class map. No hex literals, no arbitrary Tailwind values.
expectations: The output uses design-system tokens or Tailwind palette utilities (bg-green-50, bg-yellow-50, bg-red-50) inside a Record<Status, string> class map — never a hex literal like bg-[#15B79F] or style={{ background: '#...' }}
The output uses rounded-md or rounded-lg — never rounded-[6px] arbitrary values
The output references Badge from @/components/ui/badge, or recommends scaffolding it via npx shadcn@latest add badge — not a hand-rolled <span>

evals.prompt: Make a primary-looking CTA button for the onboarding hero. Should use the BONO brand pink-to-fuchsia gradient.
evals.expected_output: Use the default <Button> primitive which already carries the brand gradient. Do not hand-roll the gradient in feature code or use hex literals; reach for the primitive.
expectations: The output uses <Button> from @/components/ui/button with the default variant — it does not hand-roll a <button> with inline gradient CSS
No hex literals like #EE4379, #EC4899, or #86198F appear in the output
No arbitrary Tailwind values like bg-[linear-gradient(...)] appear in feature code — the brand gradient is encapsulated in the Button primitive
If the output mentions the token names, it uses --color-pink-500, --color-bono-500, --color-fuchsia-800 (from the Edge Design System tokens), not raw hex

evals.prompt: Add redirect-back after login so a deep-link visitor returns to the page they originally requested after authenticating.
evals.expected_output: The Login page reads a 'redirectTo' query parameter, gates it through the isSafeRedirect helper from @/lib/redirect (defined in references/router-and-layouts.md), and falls back to '/' when the value is missing or unsafe. No hand-rolled startsWith('/') check, no direct assignment of the user-supplied value to window.location.href.
expectations: The output uses isSafeRedirect from @/lib/redirect (or proposes that module if it does not yet exist) — it does not hand-roll validation with value.startsWith('/') or similar
The fallback destination when isSafeRedirect returns false is the hardcoded literal '/' — not document.referrer, history.state, or another user-influenced source
The output explicitly calls out that //evil.com, https://evil.com, and javascript: URIs are blocked by the validator

The output preserves the existing window.location.href = '/' full-reload pattern for the auth-state transition (so protectedLoader re-runs against fresh cookies) — it does not switch to useNavigate for the post-login redirect

evals.prompt: I have this component and I want to clean it up to our template's pattern: function OrdersPage() { const [orders, setOrders] = useState([]); useEffect(() => { fetch('/api/orders').then(r => r.json()).then(setOrders); }, []); return <OrderList items={orders} />; }
evals.expected_output: Apply Rule 2 from references/no-use-effect.md. Create the full vertical: src/types/order.ts, src/api/orders/ordersService.ts, src/api/orders/ordersQueryKeys.ts, src/hooks/useOrdersQuery.ts (re-exported from src/hooks/index.ts). Replace the useEffect + setState pair with the hook call. Route through httpClient, not raw fetch.
expectations: The rewritten component contains no useEffect and no raw fetch
The plan or generated code creates src/api/orders/ordersService.ts routing through httpClient from @/api/core/httpClient
The plan or generated code creates src/api/orders/ordersQueryKeys.ts with `as const` tuple keys including `all` and at least one parameterized function
The plan or generated code creates a hook file in src/hooks/ (e.g. useOrdersQuery.ts or useOrdersInfiniteQuery.ts depending on whether the endpoint pages) and re-exports from src/hooks/index.ts
The rewritten component reads { data, isPending } (or the normalized shape for infinite lists) from the hook and branches on loading state with a skeleton
The output references references/no-use-effect.md Rule 2 (TanStack Query replaces useEffect + fetch)

evals.prompt: Update our <FancyInput /> primitive to React 19 — it currently uses forwardRef. What should it look like?
evals.expected_output: React 19 ref-as-prop pattern per references/react-19-hooks.md § `ref` as a regular prop. No forwardRef wrapper. Props type includes ref?: React.Ref<HTMLInputElement>. Named export. No React.FC.
expectations: The output defines the component as a plain function: function FancyInput({ ref, ...props }: FancyInputProps) { ... }
The props type includes an optional ref field typed as React.Ref<HTMLInputElement> (or equivalent Ref<HTMLInputElement> via import type)
No forwardRef wrapper, no forwardRef import
The export is named (export function FancyInput) — not a default export
No React.FC typing on the component
The output references references/react-19-hooks.md § `ref` as a regular prop

evals.prompt: Add an optimistic 'liked' indicator to the recipes list — instantly toggle the heart when clicked, roll back on server error.
evals.expected_output: Pick one of two patterns and commit to it: (a) TanStack Query onMutate + setQueryData on recipesQueryKeys.detail or .list + onError rollback using the previous snapshot; or (b) useOptimistic for transient UI state without mutating the cache. Do not blend both. Route the server call through recipesService. No raw fetch, no hand-rolled useState + try/catch rollback.
expectations: The output chooses exactly one optimistic pattern and states why (cached server state → TanStack Query onMutate; transient pending-row UI → useOptimistic). Does not blend setQueryData and useOptimistic on the same record.

If TanStack Query is chosen: the mutation's onMutate cancels in-flight queries, snapshots previous via queryClient.getQueryData, calls queryClient.setQueryData with the optimistic value, returns { previous }; onError restores from context.previous; onSettled invalidates the affected key.
If useOptimistic is chosen: setOptimisticState is wrapped in startTransition and the pending item carries a visual indicator (e.g. opacity 0.6) without being written into the query cache.
No hand-rolled useState + try/catch rollback pattern appears in the output
The server call routes through a feature service (recipesService.toggleLike or similar) using httpClient — no direct fetch
The output references references/react-19-hooks.md § `useOptimistic`

evals.prompt: React 19 compiler handles memoization now, right? Remove all useMemo and useCallback calls from src/components/invoices/InvoiceListItem.tsx — they're noise.
evals.expected_output: Before recommending removal, check vite.config.ts for babel-plugin-react-compiler. If the plugin is NOT installed, the compiler is not running and existing useMemo/useCallback calls for measurably expensive values remain acceptable. Do not preemptively strip them — that is a performance regression. Cite references/react-19-hooks.md § Compiler trust.
expectations: The output checks (or asks the user to confirm) whether babel-plugin-react-compiler is installed in vite.config.ts before recommending removal
If the plugin is absent, the output explicitly declines to strip useMemo/useCallback and warns that removal without the compiler is a performance regression
If the plugin is present, the output recommends removal only for cases where profiling (React DevTools Profiler) shows no measurable impact — not blanket removal
The output references references/react-19-hooks.md § Compiler trust
The output does not blanket-recommend removal based on the React 19 release notes alone

evals.prompt: Rewrite the password-change form using useActionState and <form action={submitAction}>. It's the modern React 19 way — let's modernize.
evals.expected_output: Refuse to adopt useActionState in the template. Cite the template-level ban and the three concrete reasons (per-field error-code branching, clear-on-change UX, errorService coordination). Point to references/forms.md § Why not useActionState. Offer useFormStatus for the submit button as a compatible partial adoption if the user wants to reduce prop-drilling.
expectations: The output does NOT produce a useActionState-based form. No `const [state, submitAction, isPending] = useActionState(...)` and no `<form action={submitAction}>` appear in the generated code.
The output explicitly cites the template-level decision and names all three rationale reasons (per-field error-code branching, clear-on-change UX, and errorService coordination)
The output directs the user to raise a template-level RFC with the frontend tech lead rather than applying useActionState feature-locally
The output offers useFormStatus as a compatible alternative for reducing prop-drilling on the submit button, and notes that it does not require <form action>
If the output provides code, it follows the Settings.tsx pattern: useState per field, useState<FieldErrors>, validate(), mutation.mutate with onSuccess code-branching

evals.prompt: Write a Next.js server action that creates a user record and revalidates the users list.
evals.expected_output: Next.js App Router + server actions is a different stack from this skill's React Router 7 + TanStack Query + Vite target. The assistant should produce Next.js-flavored output (use server directive, revalidatePath, etc.) or clarify the stack mismatch — it MUST NOT apply this skill's 6-step flow.
expectations: The output does not apply the 6-step feature flow from this skill (types → API → hook → components → page → route)
The output does not reference useInfiniteQuery, InfiniteScrollList, httpClient from @/api/core/httpClient, or any @/components/* path from this skill
The output either uses Next.js primitives ('use server' directive, revalidatePath, revalidateTag) or flags that the target stack is Next.js, not React Router 7 + Vite
The output does not route the mutation through errorService.handleApiError or the httpClient wrapper from this skill

evals.prompt: Add an invoice detail page at /invoices/:id showing the full record, with a back-to-list link. Single record read, no pagination.
evals.expected_output: A detail page using useQuery (NOT useInfiniteQuery) keyed by invoicesQueryKeys.detail(id). Route with `:id` param under the protected AppLayout tree. useParams<{ id: string }>() + parsePositiveInt to coerce. Skeleton while pending, RouteErrorBoundary catches loader failures.
expectations: The output creates a src/hooks/useInvoiceQuery.ts (or similar single-record name) using useQuery — NOT useInfiniteQuery — with queryKey invoicesQueryKeys.detail(id) and staleTime: 0 for mutable records
The output creates src/pages/InvoiceDetail.tsx that reads the id via useParams<{ id: string }>() and coerces with parsePositiveInt from @/lib/utils
The output destructures isPending (not isLoading) from useQuery and branches to a skeleton while pending
The output adds a sibling route { path: 'invoices/:id', element: <InvoiceDetail /> } under the existing protected AppLayout children in src/router.tsx
The output routes the service call through httpClient.get<Invoice>(`/api/invoices/${id}`) — never raw fetch
The output does not wrap the page in a second ErrorBoundary (AppLayout already wraps <Outlet />) and does not add a per-route errorElement (it is inherited from the parent)

evals.prompt: Add a create-invoice form at /invoices/new that submits a new record and returns the user to the list on success.
evals.expected_output: A new page following the Settings.tsx form convention: useState per field, useState<FieldErrors>, validate() returning boolean, useCreateInvoice mutation hook, invalidate invoicesQueryKeys.all on success, useNavigate('/invoices'), errorService on error. Route added under protected AppLayout.
expectations: The output creates src/hooks/useCreateInvoice.ts as a bare useMutation({ mutationFn: invoicesService.createInvoice }) — no toast, no invalidation, no errorService inside the hook
The output extends invoicesService with a createInvoice method using httpClient.post — never raw fetch
The form uses plain useState per field and useState<FieldErrors>({}) — no useActionState, no react-hook-form
On success, the page calls queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: invoicesQueryKeys.all }) and navigates back to /invoices via useNavigate — not window.location.href
The onError branch goes through errorService.handleApiError(normalizeError(err), { operation: 'save' }) — or flags that the Operation union must be extended with a new kind like 'create' first
The submit <Button> uses isLoading={mutation.isPending} with loadingText — no custom spinner
A route entry { path: 'invoices/new', element: <InvoiceCreate /> } is added under the protected AppLayout children, alphabetical with the existing invoices route

evals.prompt: Add an invoices page to this frontend. The project tree shows src/api/core/httpClient.ts, src/lib/queryClient.ts, src/services/errorService.ts, src/components/ui/button.tsx, src/components/list/InfiniteScrollList.tsx, src/styles/main.css (with @theme block), vite.config.ts, and components.json are all present.
evals.expected_output: Prerequisite check passes. The assistant proceeds directly to the 6-step flow (types → API → hook → components → page → route) without re-scaffolding shared infrastructure. No gap report, no stop-and-ask — the gate is there to prevent re-scaffolding, not to block the happy path.
expectations: The assistant does NOT report missing prerequisites — all listed files satisfy the gate
The assistant does NOT re-create httpClient, queryClient, errorService, or InfiniteScrollList — it imports them from the existing paths
The assistant proceeds with the 6-step flow: creates src/types/invoice.ts, src/api/invoices/{service,queryKeys}.ts, src/hooks/useInvoicesInfiniteQuery.ts, components, page, and route entry
The output references the existing shared pieces by their documented paths (@/api/core/httpClient, @/lib/queryClient, @/services/errorService) rather than re-declaring them

evals.prompt: Add an invoices page to this frontend. Here is the current project tree — note that src/hooks/index.ts, src/lib/queryClient.ts, and src/components/list/InfiniteScrollList.tsx are all missing.
evals.expected_output: Prerequisite check fires. The skill's 'All present → proceed; Any missing → stop and surface the gap' rule should cause the assistant to stop and tell the user which files are missing, rather than re-scaffolding them.
expectations: The assistant identifies that prerequisite files are missing and lists them explicitly (src/hooks/index.ts, src/lib/queryClient.ts, src/components/list/InfiniteScrollList.tsx)
The assistant does not attempt to re-scaffold httpClient, queryClient, or InfiniteScrollList itself
The assistant asks the user whether to bootstrap the shared pieces first, or stops cleanly with a gap report
The assistant does not proceed to write src/types/invoice.ts, a service, or a page in the same response — the gate blocks forward motion

evals.prompt: I need a screen that shows customer records. Let me filter by status.
evals.expected_output: Oblique / vague phrasing of the Items-style infinite-list pattern. The output should apply the 6-step flow and use the template's URL-driven filter idiom (useSearchParams, InfiniteScrollList) rather than a bespoke hand-rolled list — the trigger description covers 'add a screen', 'filter', and 'list' phrasings even when stack is unnamed.
expectations: The output creates the 6-step feature vertical for customers: types/customer.ts, api/customers/{service,queryKeys}.ts, hooks/useCustomersInfiniteQuery.ts, components/customers/*, pages/Customers.tsx, router entry, header nav
The output uses <InfiniteScrollList> from @/components/list/InfiniteScrollList — not a hand-rolled map over items with window-scroll listeners
The output persists the status filter via useSearchParams (NOT only local useState) so the URL is shareable
The output routes through httpClient and centralizes query keys in customersQueryKeys.ts — never inline query keys at the useInfiniteQuery call site

evals.prompt: Add a paginated customers list with numbered page controls — explicit next/previous, 20 per page, user picks a page. Not infinite scroll.
evals.expected_output: A paginated list (not infinite) using useQuery keyed by customersQueryKeys.list({ pageIndex, pageSize }). Page index in useSearchParams. Numeric pager component. Do not use useInfiniteQuery, do not use InfiniteScrollList — those are for infinite scroll.
expectations: The hook uses useQuery (not useInfiniteQuery) with queryKey customersQueryKeys.list({ pageIndex, pageSize }) and a standard queryFn returning a single page
The output uses useSearchParams to persist pageIndex and (optionally) pageSize so refresh and back/forward preserve the page
The output does NOT use InfiniteScrollList — the renderer is a plain map with a separate numeric pager component
The output routes through httpClient.post('/api/customers/list', filter) with StartIndex and MaxResults derived from pageIndex * pageSize — matches PagedFilter contract
The output flags that useInfiniteQuery + InfiniteScrollList is the other template pattern, so the page component signals intent by choosing the paginated path explicitly
